title: Clinical utility of paced breathing as a concentration meditation practice.

assets.abstract: The present study examined changes in electroencephalogram (EEG) and heart rate variability (HRV) parameters during paced breathing (PB) and their relationships with self-reported personality traits. Fifty-eight meditation-naive subjects (36 men, 22 women) ranging in age from 20 to 30 years completed the Temperament and Character Inventory (TCI). After a spontaneous breathing session, participants were asked to breathe in 6-s cycles, guided by an acoustic stimulus. EEG, HRV, and respiratory data

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Visionary cosmologies — self-transcendence, mystical experience, numinous, awe, cosmic consciousness.
